label: Critical
definition: Immediate risk to patient safety or major regulatory violation; system must be halted
containment_action: Suspend ClinicalCompanion in affected facilities; notify users; preserve logs; activate full response team; notify regulators if required (Art. 62)
examples: Harmful suggestion acted upon; mass PII breach; complete system failure in active clinical setting

label: High
definition: Potential risk to patient safety or significant compliance issue; urgent action required
containment_action: Restrict affected functionality; increase monitoring; notify facility admins; begin root cause analysis
examples: Hallucination rate spike >10%; bias-related misdiagnosis pattern; de-ID pipeline false negatives

label: Medium
definition: Degraded performance or moderate compliance concern; prompt action needed
containment_action: Implement temporary guardrails; increase monitoring for affected metrics; schedule fix in next sprint
examples: Accuracy drops below threshold; vendor API intermittent failures; data drift exceeding limits

label: Low
definition: Minor issue with limited impact; scheduled resolution acceptable
containment_action: Log incident; track in backlog; address in standard development cycle
examples: Isolated hallucination report; minor latency increases; single physician feedback complaint
